default.value: Single-user middleware requirement: Auto-provide default user ID for all API requests. No authentication needed. System defaults to 'single-user' context. Components: middleware layer, user context manager, API interceptor. No external auth deps. Security: simplified for single-user mode.
Redis connection failure causing XHR polling errors. Redis service not running, causing ECONNREFUSED errors on agent-feed service. Need to implement graceful fallback for missing Redis.

Enhanced agent manager with 3 tabs (Production, Development, Unified), filtering by system type, showing active/inactive agents, WebSocket integration for real-time updates, agent activation/deactivation controls

Analyzed existing codebase: Found 4 Claude command variants (1. prod/claude 2. skip-permissions 3. skip-permissions -c 4. skip-permissions --resume). Current implementation uses partial child_process.spawn() but needs complete real process architecture. Frontend uses HTTP/SSE for communication. Backend has activeProcesses Map for tracking.
Analyzed current PostCreator, QuickPost, Comment components and MentionInput - found existing @ mention system with mock data, needs API integration and autocomplete enhancement
Completed SPARC Specification and Pseudocode analysis for @ mention system - created 4 comprehensive phase documents covering requirements, algorithms, architecture, and testing strategy

sse-debug.value: Frontend SSE Interface Analysis: EnhancedSSEInterface uses useSSEClaudeInstance hook to connect to instances. Messages are processed in useEffect (lines 182-201) converting terminal output to chat messages. Key finding: Claude AI responses should be coming through SSE output stream but are not reaching frontend UI. The component correctly processes 'output' type messages from SSE stream into chat messages.

Backend SSE Analysis: Backend has comprehensive SSE implementation with multiple broadcast functions: broadcastIncrementalOutput(), broadcastToConnections(), safelyBroadcastOutput(). Claude AI responses ARE being generated and should be broadcast via SSE. Key issue: Line 955 shows SSE fallback only used when NO WebSocket connections. This may be causing Claude responses to not reach frontend via SSE.

Frontend Hook Analysis: useSSEClaudeInstance hook uses SSEClaudeInstanceManager. Key message processing: handleInstanceOutput() callback (lines 94-114) receives content and creates InstanceOutputMessage objects. Messages only added to output state if instanceId matches selectedInstanceId. Event is triggered by 'instance:output' event from manager.

🚨 CRITICAL ISSUE IDENTIFIED: Backend Line 955 shows SSE fallback only used when NO WebSocket connections exist. Frontend SSE Manager has robust message handling in handleSSEMessage() (lines 378-436) but may not be receiving messages. Root cause: Backend prioritizes WebSocket over SSE, but frontend only uses SSE. Message flow broken between backend broadcast logic and frontend SSE consumption.

🧪 SWARM TEST RESULTS: SSE connection establishes successfully (Status: 200) and receives initial connection messages. However, backend server appears to shut down during processing. Key finding: Command input reaches backend and triggers pipe-based Claude AI processing, but server shuts down before response can be broadcast via SSE. This explains why Claude AI responses don't reach frontend.
